content:
Such an incredible week in Santa Barbara, CA with the DAC International team. Grateful for the chance to roll up my sleeves on-site and invest even deeper in this world-class business!

This trip was all about strategic alignment and execution. I had the opportunity to lead working sessions with our CEO, Janet Boudreau Ceddia and the President of DAC, Ken Payne, mapping out how we continue to scale DAC’s impact across the global #contactlens and #IOL #manufacturing ecosystem. We left with clear priorities, a sharper GTM & customer first focus, and even more conviction in the road ahead.

What impressed me most (again) was seeing the craftsmanship, engineering rigor, and pride that goes into every single lathe on the floor. From frames in fabrication to fully configured systems ready for FAT, the attention to detail at each station is exactly why customers trust DAC as the standard in #lathing equipment for soft lenses, GP lenses, specialty designs, and IOLs.

Huge thank you to the entire DAC International team for the hospitality, transparency, and passion you bring to your work every day. The innovation happening inside this facility is what enables our customers to push the boundaries of what’s possible in vision care.

When it comes to the contact lens and IOL industry, DAC is truly the benchmark. No other lathe consistently delivers the same level of precision, surface quality, and repeatability as a DAC lathe, and that’s why leading manufacturers around the world continue to trust DAC as their standard.

#WeAreAddtronics 

www.DAC-Intl.Com

www.Addtronics.Com

content:
12 students confirmed for GTM Engineering Cohort 01 in less than 10 days.



Different roles. Different companies. Different countries. Almost identical questions.

Who is in the room:
An SDR leader with an 8-person team. Mandate: double pipeline without adding headcount.

A first GTM hire at a stealth AI-native startup. 8 weeks to launch, building outbound from scratch.

A 9-year sales veteran mid AI-existential moment. Wants optionality. Keep the FT seat, add consulting income, keep the in-house GTME door open.

A founder tired of watching an agency burn $1,500 of Clay credits with nothing to show. Ready to own the fundamentals themselves.

A head of sales underutilizing their founder's LinkedIn network. 1-2 meetings a month from a channel that could produce 15.

A senior operator running his own AI enrichment stack. Asking: "Is GTM Engineering dying in 18 months?"

A senior SDR at 130% quota for 4 quarters straight. Already at the ceiling. No AE path visible for 24 months.

Every one of them is asking, in slightly different words:

How do I do more without hiring more?

How do I stop being replaceable by AI?

How do I keep my seat AND build the next thing on the side?

How do I own the fundamentals instead of outsourcing them?

How do I go from "recommends systems" to "builds systems"?

Same question. Different seats.

What I have learned in 10 days of enrollment calls:

The SDR ceiling is real. 130% quota is the start of a hard conversation about "what next", not the finish line

Everyone is nervous about AI. Especially the top performers. The ones asking "am I next?" are the exact ones who will not be

Founders are done outsourcing outbound. They want to own the fundamentals, then decide what to delegate

"Optionality" is the word people say out loud. Freelance + FT + agency + in-house GTME. They want all four doors open

Why this is happening now:

GTM is the last big function that has not been rebuilt around AI as an operating layer. Product, engineering, marketing, ops have all had their AI moment. Sales is next.

The people who build that operating layer in the next 12 months become the people the market rewards for the next 5 years.

That is the thesis.

If any of the 7 profiles sounded like you, you might already be one of the people who should be in the room.

We extended the deadline to Saturday.

This is being taught by one of the smartest minds in GTM engineering Shubh Saxena

People enrolling have already seen his work and they know what hes capable of.

Fill the form in the comments and I will personally reach out

#sales #gtm
